title: How Helm's Go-template pipeline renders a chart
description: Explains the values context (.Values/.Release/.Chart), pipelines, the difference between template and include, and whitespace control.
body: Use when reasoning about how a chart turns into manifests. Do not confuse template (renders, not pipeable) with include (returns a string you can pipe to nindent/indent). Actions: know that Helm builds a context (.Values from merged values, .Release, .Chart, .Capabilities), evaluates Go templates with Sprig functions, and that {{- / -}} trim whitespace while nindent fixes block indentation. Inspect with helm template --debug. Validation signals include the rendered YAML being well-formed and indentation matching the manifest structure. Risk boundary: none for rendering; the rendered output is what the cluster receives.
